conjecture_title: Near-sunflower analogue of the Erdős-Rado Sunflower Conjecture
summary: The conjecture that every k-uniform family containing no near-sunflower of size r satisfies |F| ≤ C^k (C depending only on r) remains open. The source paper, published in Israel Journal of Mathematics (2023), proves the C^k bound only under the strictly stronger condition of being focal-free (Section 6), leaving the near-sunflower version explicitly open. A 2024 follow-up (Huang–Shangguan–Zhang–Zhao, arXiv:2410.23611) provides asymptotically optimal bounds for focal-free hypergraphs and codes, extending the Alon-Holzman focal-free analysis, but does not address the near-sunflower conjecture. No verified proof or counterexample for the full conjecture has been found.

since_posted.title: Focal-free uniform hypergraphs and codes
since_posted.authors: Xinqi Huang, Chong Shangguan, Xiande Zhang, Yuhao Zhao
since_posted.claim: Gives asymptotically optimal bounds on the maximum size of focal-free uniform hypergraphs and codes (a strictly stronger condition than near-sunflower-free, as every near-sunflower-free family is also focal-free), and connects focal-free structures to the Erdős Matching Conjecture, but does not resolve the near-sunflower conjecture.

search_queries: near-sunflower k-uniform family Erdős-Rado sunflower conjecture Alon Holzman 2020
notes: The Erdős-Rado sunflower conjecture itself remains open; arXiv:2212.13609 (Fukuyama, 2022) claiming a proof was found to contain errors and was not accepted. Since the near-sunflower conjecture is weaker (near-sunflower-free is a stronger condition than sunflower-free), it remains open independently. The source paper was published as: Israel Journal of Mathematics, 2023. No internal references were supplied for verification.
